## Purpose
This file serves as a configuration for the TypeScript compiler, specifying how TypeScript files should be compiled and what options to use during the compilation process.

## User-Visible Behavior
Users will observe that when they run the TypeScript compiler, it will compile TypeScript files located in the `src` directory according to the specified options, producing output in the `dist` directory.

## Business Rules
The configuration enforces strict type checking, enables ES module interoperability, and ensures consistent casing in file names. It also specifies that JSON modules can be resolved and that type definitions for Node.js are included.

## Data Inputs And Outputs
The input to this configuration includes TypeScript files located in the `src` directory. The output is the compiled JavaScript files generated in the `dist` directory.

## Side Effects
The compilation process will generate JavaScript files in the specified output directory, which may overwrite existing files.

## Error And Edge Cases
If the TypeScript files contain errors, the compiler will report these errors, preventing successful compilation. Additionally, if the `src` directory is empty or contains no valid TypeScript files, no output will be generated.

## Dependencies
This configuration relies on the TypeScript compiler and assumes the presence of Node.js type definitions.

## Operational Notes
The configuration is set to skip type checking for library files, which can improve compilation speed. It is also designed to work with ES2022 features, so the runtime environment must support these features for the compiled code to function correctly.
